title: many apiv2 test cases don't work well on RHEL 8.4
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

/kind feature

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. make all

2. make localapiv2

**Describe the results you received:**
```
# make localapiv2 > localapiv2.log 2>&1
# grep ok localapiv2.log | wc -l
277

# grep "not ok" localapiv2.log | wc -l
123

# grep "not ok" localapiv2.log | head -5
not ok 1 [01-basic] GET /_ping : status
not ok 3 [01-basic] HEAD /_ping : status
not ok 4 [01-basic] GET /libpod/_ping : status
not ok 6 [01-basic] HEAD /libpod/_ping : status
not ok 7 [01-basic] GET _ping : status

# grep ok localapiv2.log | tail -5
ok 273 [22-stop] GET libpod/containers/mytop/json: .State.Status~\(exited\|stopped\) # skip - wrong return code
not ok 274 [22-stop] DELETE libpod/containers/mytop : status
ok 275 [23-containersArchive] HEAD containers/nonExistentCtr/archive?path=%2F : status=404
ok 276 [23-containersArchive] HEAD containers/ArchiveTestingCtr/archive?path=%2Fnon%2Fexistent%2Fpath : status=404
not ok 277 [23-containersArchive] HEAD containers/ArchiveTestingCtr/archive?path=%2Fetc%2Fpasswd : status
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
fix them.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
# ./bin/podman version
Version:      3.0.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.7
Git Commit:   69ddbde9835159b19fab33f1dd22353e4b1ca484
Built:        Sun Feb  7 08:08:12 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
# ./bin/podman info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.19.3
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v1
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.0.26-1.module+el8.4.0+9804+5385893b.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.26, commit: b9f0b7554d3ecb30977357d39b2d570ac7ecb4e6'
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: '"rhel"'
    version: "8.4"
  eventLogger: file
  hostname: ibm-x3650m4-01-vm-05.ibm2.lab.eng.bos.redhat.com
  idMappings:
    gidmap: null
    uidmap: null
  kernel: 4.18.0-280.el8.dt4.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 557146112
  memTotal: 3918069760
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-0.17-1.module+el8.4.0+9804+5385893b.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 0.17
      commit: 0e9229ae34caaebcb86f1fde18de3acaf18c6d9a
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: false
    seccompEnabled: true
    selinuxEnabled: true
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 4257214464
  swapTotal: 4257214464
  uptime: 5h 12m 43.89s (Approximately 0.21 days)
registries:
  search:
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.redhat.io
  - docker.io
store:
  configFile: /etc/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions:
    overlay.mountopt: nodev,metacopy=on
  graphRoot: /var/lib/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: xfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "true"
  imageStore:
    number: 0
  runRoot: /run/containers/storage
  volumePath: /var/lib/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.0.0
  Built: 1612703292
  BuiltTime: Sun Feb  7 08:08:12 2021
  GitCommit: 69ddbde9835159b19fab33f1dd22353e4b1ca484
  GoVersion: go1.15.7
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.0.0-dev
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
# git rev-parse HEAD
69ddbde9835159b19fab33f1dd22353e4b1ca484
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
# cat /etc/redhat-release
Red Hat Enterprise Linux release 8.4 Beta (Ootpa)

comments.body: @chuanchang isn't there any process using 8081 on you machine by any chance?

> @chuanchang isn't there any process using 8081 on you machine by any chance?
The port 8081 is used by restraintd on the RHEL-8, I tried to kill restraintd process, 
and it works well for me now, 

```
# netstat -lanp|grep 8081
tcp        0      0 127.0.0.1:8081          0.0.0.0:*               LISTEN      5109/restraintd
tcp6       0      0 ::1:8081                :::*                    LISTEN      5109/restraintd

# grep ok localapiv2.log |wc -l
586

# grep "not ok" localapiv2.log |wc -l
1
# grep "not ok" localapiv2.log
not ok 41 [01-basic] GET info : .DefaultRuntime

NOTE: the crun is defalut runtime on RHEL-8.4, so it's an expected failure.
```
